subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Children and Water
description: A micro-lesson that makes Children and Water usable.

hook.text: A toddler can drown in 2 inches of water—a bucket, a toilet, a puddle. They fall in face-first.

buildup.text: Drowning is the leading cause of death for children ages 1-4 in the United States.

discovery.text: Toddlers are top-heavy—their large heads make them tip forward into water and they can't right themselves.

twist.text: 69% of drowning deaths in children under 4 occur during non-swim times—they weren't expected near water.

climax.text: Constant adult supervision—within arm's reach—is the only reliable protection for young children.

punchline.text: Touch supervision. Arm's reach. Eyes on the child. Not the phone.

quiz.question: Why are toddlers particularly vulnerable to drowning?
options: They're top-heavy and tip into water face-first
They are attracted to blue colors
